memories.title: Maestro route tests must cover new project_id resolution/error cases
memories.body: Daemon `maestro` tests don’t cover the new `resolveProjectScope` behavior yet; focused cases for registered resolution, project_id_required rejection, and fallback warning paths are required.

memories.title: Commit message for fix-set
memories.body: Implemented and committed as requested. Commit: `35b6a08` (`fix(agentchat,daemon): propagate project_id through retry and maestro paths`).

memories.title: Silent-failure-retry helper must be type-safe with mainRepoDir
memories.body: The new `adhoc` tests reference `daemon.services.mainRepoDir` directly, which can be absent; thread `mainRepoDir` from the test daemon factory to avoid type errors.

memories.title: MCP bypasses package boundary into core/src
memories.body: MCP is directly reaching into `core/src` instead of using the intended package boundary, which violates the intended modular layering and could lead to tight coupling and scaling issues.

memories.title: Dispatch Routing Guard Refactor to Registry-Driven Path Extraction
memories.body: The dispatch-routing guard was refactored to use registry iteration for path extraction instead of a host-specific absolute-path regex. This change includes exporting helper functions and adding focused unit tests to verify the new logic. The refactor ensures that nested `/...` segments after a registered root are correctly consumed, and unnecessary variables in the test file were removed to satisfy stricter TypeScript settings. The changes were verified with `bun run typecheck`, core unit tests, and daemon sanity tests, resulting in a clean commit (ef0f1a3).

memories.title: Test-Driven Development Workflow for Scoped Changes
memories.body: For scoped code changes requiring verification, the workflow involves inspecting existing implementation and tests, applying targeted patches, adjusting test harness specifics (e.g., Bun's separate `describe` export), and performing sequential verification with typecheck, focused tests, and daemon tests. This approach ensures that only relevant files are committed and stray worktree issues are acknowledged without affecting the change set.

memories.title: MCP request body typing alignment
memories.body: The MCP request body must be typed against the daemon client method signature rather than using a generic `any` or `z.input` quirk. This ensures the request fields match the expected schema, eliminating boundary mismatches and improving type safety.

memories.title: Silent-failure-retry helper must be type-safe with mainRepoDir
memories.body: The new `adhoc` tests reference `daemon.services.mainRepoDir` directly, which can be absent; thread `mainRepoDir` from the test daemon factory to avoid type errors.

memories.title: MCP request body typing alignment

memories.title: Internal-hop predicate tightening for null project IDs
memories.body: Resolved parent records with a null project ID must not be treated as internal hops. The predicate in the handoff reader must correctly differentiate between null and undefined values to avoid treating absent parents as internal, preventing unintended internal-hop behavior in the daemon route.

memories.title: Daemon response schema parsing enforcement
memories.body: After receiving a daemon response, it must be parsed before use to ensure the response adheres to the expected schema. This prevents runtime errors from unexpected response shapes and maintains contract integrity.

memories.title: Test-first bugfix discipline
memories.body: Implement bug fixes by first writing failing coverage, then making the smallest code change that satisfies the failing tests, followed by rerunning targeted tests and the repository-wide typecheck. This ensures changes are verified and isolated.

memories.body: The registry test setup was modified to guarantee that any symlinks referenced during `realpathSync` resolution have their targets present, preventing false negatives in the canonical path verification.

memories.title: Registry entries now include canonical root paths
memories.body: The project now enriches registry entries in-memory by computing a canonical root path using `realpathSync()` for each `root_path`. This canonical path is stored as `canonical_root_path` and is used by the guard for accurate matching.

memories.title: Tri-state return for project ID resolution
memories.body:
`resolveProjectIdFromAbsolutePath` now returns a tri-state result:
- `{ status: 'unique', project_id }`
- `{ status: 'ambiguous', candidate_ids }`
- `{ status: 'not_found' }`
This change addresses ambiguity in path resolution at 50 writes/sec and aligns with the guard logic that previously collapsed ambiguity to `undefined`.

memories.title: Registry logger sanitization and deduplication
memories.body: The registry logger now sanitizes interpolated values and caps the dedupe set to prevent unbounded growth on repeated reloads. Missing-file warnings also reuse this sanitized interpolation logic.

memories.title: Dispatch routing guard update
memories.body: Orchestrate dispatches must now use `trust=all` to ensure the tri-state return from `resolveProjectIdFromAbsolutePath` is correctly propagated through the guard logic.

memories.title: SQL filter added to block self-feeding summary rows in workflows
memories.body: A SQL filter was implemented in `packages/daemon/src/routes/workflows.ts` (line 230) to prevent summary events from self-feeding. This change was accompanied by a regression test in `packages/daemon/test/routes/workflows.test.ts` that seeds a workflow-sourced summary event into a real in-memory database and asserts that no draft is written, ensuring the real DB reader path is exercised rather than the extractor mock.

memories.title: Workflow test and typecheck passed successfully
memories.body: After implementing the SQL filter and adding the regression test, the focused workflow test file and a subsequent typecheck both reported green results, confirming that the change did not introduce new issues and correctly blocks the self-feeding summary row.

memories.title: Repository guidance followed before code modification
memories.body: The assistant first consulted repository guidance and inspected the workflow route and its existing tests to ensure the smallest necessary change was made, specifically targeting the real database path rather than relying on mocks.

memories.title: Trust-boundary issue in `dispatch_kind='workflow'`
memories.body: The code allows client-controlled `dispatch_kind='workflow'` to bypass strict project-routing enforcement, creating a high-impact security bypass.

memories.title: Lane-close auto-fire helper implementation
memories.body: A new lane-close auto-fire helper was added in `packages/daemon/src/workers/handoff-sweeper.ts`. The helper is parameterized by the closed lane’s verdict and is wired into the lane-close finalization seam. A focused worker test was written to verify the helper's signature and DB query, followed by a full repository test run to ensure no unrelated baseline failures were introduced. The change was committed after confirming the focused regressions were clean.

memories.title: Lane-close auto-fire helper implementation
memories.body: A new lane-close auto-fire helper was added in `packages/daemon/src/workers/handoff-sweeper.ts`. The helper is parameterized by the closed lane’s verdict and is wired into the lane-close finalization seam. A focused worker test was written to verify the helper's signature and DB query, followed by a full repository test run to ensure no unrelated baseline failures were introduced. The change was committed after confirming the focused regressions were clean.

memories.title: Commit process adherence
memories.body: Only the files directly affected by the lane-close hook, the worker helper, and the new regression test were staged and committed. This adheres to the hard-stop rule of not committing unrelated changes.

memories.title: Testing strategy for new helper
memories.body: A dedicated failing test was written first to confirm the missing auto-fire path. After the failure was verified, the implementation was added and tested with a focused worker test before expanding to the full suite. The full suite was run to catch any unrelated baseline failures.

memories.title: Requirement for lane-verdict context in auto-fire helper
memories.body: The auto-fire helper needed explicit lane-verdict context from the lane-close path, not just a handoff ID. This context was added to the implementation to ensure correct behavior, as discovered during the debugging phase.

memories.title: Server-Issued Workflow Run ID Gate Implementation
memories.body: Implemented a server-issued `workflow_run_id` gate for `dispatch_kind='workflow'`. This involves adding an in-memory `WorkflowRunsRegistry` with TTL and size cap located at `packages/daemon/src/services/workflow-runs-registry.ts`. The registry is used to generate and validate `workflow_run_id` tokens, ensuring they are only granted in the guarded branch and preventing spoofing. The fix required updating the workflow context API to accept `workflowRunId`, wiring the registry into the daemon bootstrap, and adjusting related tests and type declarations to reflect the new field and behavior.

memories.title: Workflow Run Registry Must Require Concrete project_id
memories.body: The workflow-run registry now enforces a concrete `project_id` in its types and callers only mint a run ID when the resolved project is non-null. This prevents the chain routing guard from treating `null` project IDs as equivalent to the request project.

memories.title: Mocked Global-Scope Resolver for Workflow Route Tests
memories.body: To accurately test the workflow route's handling of global scope without filesystem heuristics, a deterministic mocked resolver was introduced. This ensures the test exercises the exact branch the fix targets.

memories.title: Worktree Manager Isolation via PENUMBRA_WORKTREE_ROOT
memories.body: Setting `PENUMBRA_WORKTREE_ROOT` to an empty temporary directory disables the worktree manager's scanning of shared workspaces, eliminating noisy side effects during focused test runs.

memories.title: Chain Routing Guard Tightened for Null Project IDs
memories.body: The chain routing guard now explicitly rejects `null` project IDs, enforcing that only valid, non-null project identifiers are processed. This guard was previously bypassed, allowing null-scoped tokens to pass through.

memories.title: Repository-Level Verification Process
memories.body: After code changes, a repository-level verification process runs `bun typecheck` followed by a core rebuild to confirm no unintended leaks or interface regressions occurred.

memories.title: Workflow-run hardening: project-scoped authorization and persistence
memories.body: The implementation now binds `workflow_status` and `workflow_wait` to the caller’s resolved project scope, returning a `not_found` shape when the run is not within that project. The registry is persisted to SQLite with an eviction policy that retains active runs and enforces bounded waits, closing the BOLA/IDOR gap and addressing capacity exhaustion explicitly.

memories.title: Worktree Manager Isolation via PENUMBRA_WORKTREE_ROOT
memories.body: Setting `PENUMBRA_WORKTREE_ROOT` to an empty temporary directory disables the worktree manager's scanning of shared workspaces, eliminating noisy side effects during focused test runs.

memories.title: Chain Routing Guard Tightened for Null Project IDs
memories.body: The chain routing guard now explicitly rejects `null` project IDs, enforcing that only valid, non-null project identifiers are processed. This guard was previously bypassed, allowing null-scoped tokens to pass through.

memories.body: The final commit (`28154d5`) bundles all source and test changes while removing untracked `node_modules` directories, ensuring a clean, focused commit history.

memories.title: Repository-Level Verification Process
memories.body: After code changes, a repository-level verification process runs `bun typecheck` followed by a core rebuild to confirm no unintended leaks or interface regressions occurred.

memories.title: Workflow-run hardening: project-scoped authorization and persistence
memories.body: The implementation now binds `workflow_status` and `workflow_wait` to the caller’s resolved project scope, returning a `not_found` shape when the run is not within that project. The registry is persisted to SQLite with an eviction policy that retains active runs and enforces bounded waits, closing the BOLA/IDOR gap and addressing capacity exhaustion explicitly.

memories.title: Registry implementation details
memories.body: The workflow-run registry is now SQLite-backed and lives in the daemon’s service builder, persisting runs and enforcing an eviction policy that keeps active runs while capping waits. The implementation required exporting registry types from the core package and adjusting the `Services` contract to handle capacity exhaustion explicitly.

memories.title: Test suite adjustments
memories.body: Tests were rewritten to use the real project-scoped registry and to exercise the new authorization and persistence behavior. This included fixing test harness issues, updating `cwd` references, and ensuring the core `Services` contract aligns with the persisted registry implementation.

memories.title: Implementation pitfalls and workarounds
memories.body: Key pitfalls included unexported core types in the registry, stale test placeholders for `cwd`, and the need for real project-scoping in auth checks. Workarounds involved exporting necessary types, updating test harnesses, and ensuring the daemon uses the same registry instance as the tests.

memories.title: Lane-close memory verification capped per tick
memories.body: The sweeper now limits memory verification work to a maximum per tick defined by PENUMBRA_MEMORY_VERIFY_MAX_PER_TICK in packages/daemon/src/workers/handoff-sweeper.ts, preventing excessive CPU usage during high write rates.

memories.title: SQLite vec loader no longer owns runtime pragmas
memories.body: The loadSqliteVec function in packages/core/src/db/sqlite-vec.ts has been refactored to delegate runtime pragmas to the CLI bootstrap paths, making pragma handling explicit and testable.

memories.title: Shared DB bootstrap paths updated for pragma policy
memories.body: Bootstrap paths in the shared DB initialization now reference the explicit pragma application, ensuring consistency across all database openings.

memories.title: SQLite vec loader no longer owns runtime pragmas

memories.title: Shared DB bootstrap paths updated for pragma policy
memories.body: Bootstrap paths in the shared DB initialization now reference the explicit pragma application, ensuring consistency across all database openings.

memories.title: Dispatch-event-log truncation made strict
memories.body: Dispatch event log truncation now fails loudly for strict event kinds, ensuring malformed events are caught early rather than silently truncated.

memories.title: Shared DB bootstrap paths updated for pragma policy
memories.body: Bootstrap paths in the shared DB initialization now reference the explicit pragma application, ensuring consistency across all database openings.

memories.title: Dispatch-event-log truncation made strict
memories.body: Dispatch event log truncation now fails loudly for strict event kinds, ensuring malformed events are caught early rather than silently truncated.

memories.title: Tests added for pragma split and strict-kind rejection
memories.body: Focused tests verify that loadSqliteVec no longer sets pragmas, and that dispatch-event-log rejects non-conforming strict events, covering both behavioral changes.

memories.title: Commit created with consolidated changes
memories.body: All modifications were staged and committed as a single changeset (8b21ac7) with the message 'Cap lane-close verification, enforce strict dispatch truncation, and split SQLite pragma ownership'.

memories.title: Package discovery fallback path handling
memories.body: When the reconciler reads manifests from the worktree, it now uses `wt.path` instead of a hardcoded `deps.mainRepoDir`. This change allows dynamic discovery of `packages/*` and resolves warnings emitted by mocked worktree tests. To avoid typecheck failures, the test suite that exercises this path must be run with a real repo root as the worktree path, so the fallback sees actual package manifests.

memories.title: Gemini persona timeout root cause
memories.body: Root cause is most likely the `gemini-acp-pro` adapter's wall-clock cutoff, not prompt content and not an obvious upstream Gemini parse/refusal regression.

memories.title: Shared DB bootstrap paths updated for pragma policy
memories.body: Bootstrap paths in the shared DB initialization now reference the explicit pragma application, ensuring consistency across all database openings.

memories.title: Dispatch-event-log truncation made strict
memories.body: Dispatch event log truncation now fails loudly for strict event kinds, ensuring malformed events are caught early rather than silently truncated.

memories.title: Tests added for pragma split and strict-kind rejection
memories.body: Focused tests verify that loadSqliteVec no longer sets pragmas, and that dispatch-event-log rejects non-conforming strict events, covering both behavioral changes.

memories.title: Commit created with consolidated changes
memories.body: All modifications were staged and committed as a single changeset (8b21ac7) with the message 'Cap lane-close verification, enforce strict dispatch truncation, and split SQLite pragma ownership'.

To avoid typecheck failures, the test suite that exercises this path must be run with a real repo root as the worktree path, so the fallback sees actual package manifests.

memories.title: Gemini persona timeout root cause
memories.body: Root cause is most likely the `gemini-acp-pro` adapter's wall-clock cutoff, not prompt content and not an obvious upstream Gemini parse/refusal regression.

memories.title: Why Postgres was chosen for audit log over SQLite
memories.body: We chose Postgres over SQLite for the audit log because partitioning matters at 50 writes/sec; SQLite lacks robust partitioning capabilities at this scale.

memories.body: We chose Postgres over SQLite for the audit log because partitioning matters at 50 writes/sec; SQLite lacks robust partitioning capabilities at this scale.

memories.title: Parallel boot actions using allSettled
memories.body: Switched three boot actions (heartbeat, long-lived reconcile, and dispatch) to fire concurrently with `allSettled`, preserving per-call retry logic while ensuring dispatch starts after the sequential long-lived step yields.

memories.title: Concurrent boot flow assertions
memories.body: Tightened test assertions to reflect the concurrent boot flow: dispatch can retry, and the long-lived retry now overlaps with dispatch instead of happening before it.
